description: UI/UX Remediation — fix core product promise, responsive layout, onboarding clarity, copy quality, and trust/conversion for Facebook-sourced mobile users (Gen X, Boomers, early Millennials)

userStories.title: Prevent destructive profile saves — add required-field validation
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Save button to block saving when required fields are blank so my working plan is never overwritten with empty data.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, add a validation function: zipCode must be exactly 5 digits and grassType must be non-empty to enable save
Disable the Save Profile button (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) when validation fails

Below the ZIP input when invalid: show

Enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code

Below the Grass Type select when empty: show

Select a grass type
Error messages only appear after the user has touched the field (add touched state per field)
ZIP input: add inputMode='numeric' and autoComplete='postal-code' attributes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diagnose and fix empty generated lawn plans
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to understand why a valid Bermudagrass / Arkansas profile may produce zero tasks on the dashboard and annual calendar, and fix the root cause so the core product promise is delivered.
acceptanceCriteria: INVESTIGATE — Week convention: read getISOWeek() in src/app/dashboard/page.tsx and confirm exactly what week number it returns for June 15 2026 under the app's own logic before writing any test fixture. Do not assume ISO week 25 without verifying.

INVESTIGATE — Trace the full plan-generation path: sessionStorage onboarding_state → POST /api/parse-pdf → Anthropic SDK call → raw text → JSON parse → WeeklyPlan[] → find(w => w.week === currentWeek) → setTasks(). Read src/app/api/parse-pdf/route.ts in full.

INVESTIGATE — Identify the failure stage: (a) grass-id mismatch between grassTypes[].id and what Claude's prompt sends; (b) state/region key mismatch in extension-sources data; (c) week-number produced by getISOWeek() not matching week values in WeeklyPlan[]; (d) Claude returning malformed JSON; (e) JSON.parse throwing and being silently swallowed; (f) /api/parse-pdf 4xx/5xx swallowed by the dashboard's catch block.
TESTS — All tests must use deterministic in-process fixtures. Do NOT call Claude, Anthropic, or any external PDF service from tests.
TEST 1 — A fixture WeeklyPlan[] for AR + Bermudagrass containing tasks in multiple weeks is stored as a JSON file in __tests__/fixtures/ar-bermudagrass-plan.json.
TEST 2 — Given the fixture, the lookup getWeekPlan(plan, knownWeek) returns the correct WeeklyTask[] for that week (tests the week-lookup logic in isolation).
TEST 3 — Given the fixture and a week that has no tasks, the 'next real task' finder returns the nearest future week entry with tasks (not a hardcoded message).
TEST 4 — A malformed AI response (empty string, '{', '[]', null) is caught and results in a plan-generation error, not a zero-task success.
TEST 5 — A failed generation attempt does not overwrite a previously valid cached plan in the DB (test the cache-read-before-write logic in the API route).
TEST 6 — Grass IDs and state codes are normalized to lowercase + trimmed before lookup so 'Bermuda ' and 'BERMUDA' both match the canonical id.

LOGGING — Add server-side console.error (never client-side) at each failure stage: JSON parse failure, empty-plan result for a profile that reached Claude, week-lookup miss. Log the stage name and input values (state, grassType, week) but never user PII.
FIX — Fix the identified root cause without adding any generic advice (mowing, watering, fertilizer) that does not come from the Claude-generated plan data.

EMPTY PLAN ERROR STATE — When /api/parse-pdf returns an empty plan or the dashboard's fetch fails, show: heading 'We could not build your plan' with body 'Something went wrong generating your recommendations.' plus a Retry button (re-calls loadData) and a link to /profile labeled 'Check your lawn details'.

QUIET WEEK STATE — When the annual plan IS populated but the current week has no tasks, show the next real future task from the plan: 'Nothing due this week. Up next: [task.title] — Week [week].' with a link to the calendar. No hardcoded advice.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — confirm a Bermudagrass / AR profile either shows real tasks or the correct quiet-week or error state
userStories.notes: Root causes: (1) SYSTEM_PROMPT week numbering ambiguous — fixed with ISO 8601 spec; (2) empty plan [] silently treated as success — fixed with validateWeeklyPlan(); (3) grassType not normalized — fixed with normalizeGrassId(); (4) silent error swallowing — fixed with planError state; (5) quiet-week hardcoded copy — fixed with findNextTaskWeek(). Patch: cached plans now validated with same validator; clampWeek() handles week 53; retry preserves prior plan; client console.error removed; 47 tests across 2 files.

userStories.title: Fix calendar horizontal scroll — replace overflow-x strip with responsive grid
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the annual calendar to show without requiring horizontal scrolling, which is easy to miss and inaccessible.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/dashboard/calendar/page.tsx, replace the calendar viewMode block that uses overflow-x-auto + min-w-max + flex gap-2 with a CSS grid
New grid: <div className='grid grid-cols-3 sm:grid-cols-4 md:grid-cols-6 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-2'>
Each month card: remove the w-28 shrink-0 fixed-width classes; add w-full to each card's outer div
Month card button: min-height 80px so content has room; keep rounded-lg p-3 border styling
No overflow-x-auto or min-w-max anywhere on the calendar page
Category dot legend below the calendar stays as is
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Replaced overflow-x-auto + flex gap-2 min-w-max strip with responsive CSS grid (grid-cols-3 sm:4 md:6 lg:12). Removed w-28 shrink-0 from month cards; added w-full + minHeight 80px on the button. Typecheck, lint, and full test suite (47 tests) pass. No browser automation tool available in this environment — manual visual verification in a real browser is still recommended to confirm the grid layout and expanded-month detail panel render correctly across breakpoints.

instead of —

userStories.title: Create /faq page with common lawn-care and product questions
userStories.description: As a visitor or user, I want a FAQ page that answers common questions about the product, data storage, and lawn terminology so I can trust the site.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/faq/page.tsx as a server component (no 'use client')
Page title: 'Frequently Asked Questions' in text-3xl font-bold

Include these 8 Q&A items using an accordion-style <details>/<summary> HTML pattern (no JS needed): (1) 'What if I don't know my grass type?' (2) 'What if I don't know my lawn size?' (3) 'What is a USDA growing zone?' (4) 'What is pre-emergent herbicide?' (5) 'Is the plan free?' (6) 'How is my plan saved?' — answer: 'Your plan is saved on this device using browser storage. It will persist as long as you use the same browser and don't clear your data. We recommend bookmarking your dashboard or printing your plan.' (7) 'Is this safe around pets and children?' (8) 'Where does the advice come from?'
Each <details> has className='border-b py-4' with style borderColor var(--color-border); <summary> is cursor-pointer font-semibold text-base; answer text is text-sm var(--color-text-muted) mt-2
Max-width container: max-w-2xl mx-auto px-6 py-12
Add a link to /faq in the landing page footer (create a simple footer if none exists)
